info.name: Sync Pipeline & Metrics API
info.description: Collection for testing the data sync pipeline and metrics service. Run requests in order: trigger a sync first, then verify data, then check metrics.

variable.description: Change to your Render URL for production testing, e.g. https://sync-pipeline.onrender.com

request.description: Simple liveness check. Render uses this path to monitor the service. Returns {status: 'ok', timestamp: '...'}.
Returns the last stored cursor/syncToken per source. Use this to verify that incremental sync is progressing correctly after each trigger.

Kicks off a sync across all three sources (HubSpot, Google Calendar, Stripe) in parallel using Promise.allSettled. Returns immediately with {status: 'sync_started'} — the actual sync runs in the background. Watch the server logs for real-time progress.
Returns up to 50 most recently updated records from the unified_records table. Includes both HubSpot contacts and Google Calendar events in one normalized schema.
Filter unified records by source. Use 'hubspot' for CRM contacts or 'gcal' for calendar events.
Returns calendar events synced from Google Calendar. Each event is normalized to the same schema as HubSpot contacts.

Returns up to 50 most recent Stripe charges. Notice the 'status' field shows the canonical value (e.g. 'collected') while 'original_status' shows what Stripe actually sent (e.g. 'succeeded'). Failed charges show status='failed' and are excluded from revenue calculations.
Returns total collected revenue across all sources. Only transactions with canonical status='collected' are counted — this is a strict allow-list, not an exclusion list.
Same total revenue but filtered to a specific date range. Both startDate and endDate are optional and use YYYY-MM-DD format.

Returns revenue grouped by day. Uses the exact same base query as /metrics/total so the sum of all breakdown amounts will always equal the total. This is by design — both endpoints share one query function.
Day-by-day breakdown filtered to a date range. Sum the breakdown values and compare with /metrics/total using the same date range — they will always match.
